description:
# User-facing diagnostic — show every body and the boundary that forced it

## Goal

A diagnostic mode (`fz dump --emit bodies` or similar) that prints,
for each user function with a body in the compiled output, the
source location of the **boundary** that forced the body to exist.

## Example output shape

```
$ fz dump --emit bodies fixtures/ast_eval/input.fz
bodies emitted: 0 user functions
  (no boundaries — program fully reduces)

$ fz dump --emit bodies fixtures/tail_recursion/input.fz
bodies emitted: 1 user function
  count: 1 body (input Descr: (int, int))
    forced by: unroll budget exceeded at main:5:9 (count(100000, 0))
    → raise @unroll(N) on main or count, or rewrite to terminate

$ fz dump --emit bodies fixtures/concurrency_ping_pong/input.fz
bodies emitted: 2 user functions
  child: 1 body (zero-capture thunk)
    forced by: spawn at main:6:3
  k_main_print_cont: 1 body (input Descr: any)
    forced by: untyped receive at main:7:9 (receive())
    → declare a typed mailbox to narrow this body
```

## Acceptance

- Reports correct body counts for every fixture in the table from
  `docs/bodies-are-boundaries.md` § "What success looks like".
- Boundary attribution is correct (the listed source location is
  the one that forced the body).
- For boundaries that have a known fix (typed receive, raising the
  unroll budget, etc.), include a one-line suggestion.

## Removals

None.
title: fz-RED.7: --explain-bodies diagnostic
